---
name: end-my-day
description: 'Close the work day: stop timers, generate standup summary, commit and push worklogs, post to Teams. Use when the user says "end my day", "close out", "wrap up", "send standup", or "EOD".'
argument-hint: 'Say "end my day" to begin the shutdown sequence'
---

# End My Day Skill

Close the work day: finalize the daily note with a standup section, post the summary to Teams, and push to trigger worklog CI/CD.

## When to Use

- User says "end my day", "close out", "wrap up", "send standup", "EOD"
- End of a work session — committing time logs and sending summary

---

## Workflow

### Step 1 — Stop any running timers

Check if any `tl` timers are still running:

```bash
cd /home/wweeks/git/projects && cat planner/.timers.json 2>/dev/null || echo "no timers"
```

If timers are active, stop them:
```bash
tl stop <PROJECT>-KEY
```

Tell the user which timers were stopped and the logged duration.

### Step 2 — Ask about blockers

Before closing, ask the user if there are any blockers to document:

> "Any blockers or things you're stuck on to add to the standup?"

If none, proceed. If yes, collect the text — you'll pass it as `--blocker` args.

### Step 3 — Generate standup and close the note

```bash
cd /home/wweeks/git/projects
python3 scripts/daily_note.py --end \
  [--blocker "<PROJECT>-KEY: description"] \
  [--blocker "second blocker"]
```

This will:
1. Query Jira for completed today (`flow:done`, updated today)
2. Query Jira for waiting tickets (`flow:waiting`)
3. Query Jira for still-active tickets (`flow:active`)
4. Write the `* Standup` section into today's org file
5. Print the Teams-formatted standup message
6. Commit all changes and push (triggers `jira-worklog-sync.yaml` and `sdp-worklog-sync.yaml`)
7. Post to Teams if `TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL` is set in `.env`

### Step 4 — Present the summary

Show the user the Teams standup message. If `TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL` is not set, provide it for copy-paste.

#### Standup History Diff

Before presenting today's standup, compare it to yesterday's:

```bash
# Find yesterday's org file
YESTERDAY=$(date -d 'yesterday' +%m-%d 2>/dev/null || date -v-1d +%m-%d)
cat planner/${YESTERDAY}.org 2>/dev/null | grep -A 50 "* Standup"
```

Check for broken promises — things that were in yesterday's "Active / Up Next" that are STILL in today's active list with no progress:

```
🔄 Carried forward (no change since yesterday):
   • <PROJECT>-390 — same TODO: "Define spike scope and success criteria"
     → Consider: timebox 30m tomorrow or park it

✅ Promises kept:
   • <PROJECT>-366 — was "Up Next", now Done ✓
```

**Rules:**
- Only surface carried-forward items if the same `:NEXT:` step is unchanged
- Don't nag about tickets where progress was logged today (even if not closed)
- If 3+ consecutive days carry the same ticket with no logged time, flag it strongly:
  ```
  ⚠  <PROJECT>-390 has been "up next" for 3 days with 0 time logged.
     This might not be the right time for this work. Park it?
  ```

Tell the user:
- What was committed and pushed
- Which CI/CD workflows were triggered (worklog sync)
- Where the daily note was saved

### Step 5 — Outbox and waiting ticket check

Before final commit, verify the outbox is current and waiting tickets are synced to Jira:

```bash
cd /home/wweeks/git/projects && export $(grep -v '^#' .env | xargs)

# Refresh outbox from issue files
python3 scripts/outbox_refresh.py

# Publish outbox to Confluence (WWeeks → WWeeks Outbox)
python3 scripts/outbox_publish.py

# Sync notes/next-steps from issue files to Jira custom fields
python3 scripts/jira_sync_waiting.py
```

Surface any issues:
- Waiting tickets with a thin `## Notes` section (Lede/Status/Next) — the outbox card will be sparse and may need a Notes refresh in the source issue file
- Tickets where notes/next-steps couldn't be synced to Jira
- Stale outbox entries (>7 days with no follow-up sent)
- Any non-idempotent change from the Confluence publish (every page should be a clean version bump — no errors)

```
📬 Outbox check:
   ✓ 8 stakeholder files refreshed (michael-seaman: 38, _vendor: 7, _external-ticket: 4, _code-review: 1, andrew-fuqua: 1, greg-carlton: 1, joshua-marquard: 1, _compliance: 1)
   ✓ 8 Confluence pages published to WWeeks → WWeeks Outbox (see planner/outbox/INDEX.md)
   ⚠ <PROJECT>-392 has no `## Notes` Lede — card will be thin until refreshed
   🔴 <PROJECT>-349 (8d stale) — consider follow-up or park
```

### Step 6 — Suggest next-session prep (optional)

If any ticket is clearly ready to be closed (e.g., waiting tickets that have been resolved), offer to update flow labels now.

---

## Standup Format

The Teams message generated by the script:

```
📋 **Standup — May 23, 2026**

✅ **Completed Today**
• <PROJECT>-366 — Fabric pipeline schedule fix confirmed
• <PROJECT>-360 — Sites.Selected permission applied

⏳ **Waiting On**
• <PROJECT>-342 — azpim-prd-edmlevel1 assignment pending
• <PROJECT>-58 — Team decision on ITSM direction

🚫 **Blockers**
• None

📅 **Active / Up Next**
🔴 <PROJECT>-363 — Five9 call script engine
🔵 <PROJECT>-357 — Entra security groups
🟢 <PROJECT>-390 — Web Platform SSR spike
```

---

## Notes

- The daily note must already exist (`--start` must have been run today)
- `--no-push` flag skips the git commit if you want to review before pushing
- Blockers are passed per `--blocker "text"` — each becomes a bullet in the standup
- Teams post requires `TEAMS_WEBHOOK_URL` in `.env` — otherwise the message is printed for copy-paste
- The git push triggers `jira-worklog-sync.yaml` (issues/**) and `sdp-worklog-sync.yaml` (cases/**) via CI/CD

---

## Composes

```
end-my-day
├── jira_sync_waiting.py  (Step 5 — push notes/next-steps to Jira)
├── outbox_refresh.py     (Step 5 — rebuild stakeholder outbox files)
├── outbox_publish.py     (Step 5 — mirror outbox to Confluence WWeeks Outbox)
├── waiting-ticket-followup (Step 6 — check for tickets needing follow-up)
└── jira-set-flow           (Step 6 — close resolved waiting tickets)
```

**Called by:** `rounds` (Phase 5, on "end the day"), or directly by user

---

## Constraint Analysis (End-of-Day)

Before generating the standup, run a quick constraint audit on today's work:

1. **Technician load:** Count how many of today's active tickets had `constraint:technician`. If all 3:
   ```
   🏭 Constraint alert: All 3 active tickets today were technician-only.
      You ARE the bottleneck. Review which ones could be documented or delegated.
   ```

2. **Knowledge capture nudge:** For any ticket closed today with `constraint:technician` that does NOT already have a linked Confluence page or `way:doc` backlog item:
   ```
   📝 <PROJECT>-366 was technician-only and is now done.
      Worth a 5-min Confluence page so someone else handles it next time?
   ```

3. **Repeat blocker detection:** If today's blockers overlap with blockers from the last 3 standups (same stakeholder, same type):
   ```
   🔄 Repeat blocker: "Waiting on {name}" appeared in 3 of last 5 standups.
      → Escalation needed? Or process change to bypass this dependency?
   ```

These surface at the END of the standup — after the user sees the summary, before final commit.


---

## SDP Awareness (Lanes 4–6)

ServiceDesk Plus work runs as a parallel set of three swimlanes (Lane 4 🔴 SDP-Urgent, Lane 5 🟠 SDP-Approval, Lane 6 🟢 SDP-Background). SDP case files live under `cases/{display_id}/`. The header `OWNER: jira | sdp` decides which side holds the WIP slot:

- `OWNER: sdp` (default) — the SDP case is the WIP owner; counts against the SDP lane cap
- `OWNER: jira` — the SDP case is a **shadow** of a linked <PROJECT>-XXX issue; does NOT count against any SDP lane cap

When this skill needs to enumerate or render the operator's work, it MUST union both sources (`issues/` + `cases/`) and dedupe by cross-link (`JIRA:` header in cases, `SDP:` header in issues). Shadows are surfaced as "(shadow of <PROJECT>-XXX)" annotations, not as independent slots.

For lane-routing and rounds claims, see `rounds` and `sdp-dispatcher`. The shared `/tmp/rounds-claims.json` uses keys `lane1`–`lane5` shared across Jira and SDP tickets (single ticket per lane, regardless of system).

Voice wall: SDP `COMMENT:` lines are **end-user voice** (plain language). Jira COMMENT lines are internal investigative voice. This skill must preserve that distinction wherever it emits or summarizes comments.

For SDP-specific dispatch, render, or close-out, hand off to: `rounds`, `sdp-dispatcher`, `sdp-investigator`, `sdp-worklog`, `sdp-router`.
